info.title: nibot
info.description: A cli tool helps you to de-duplicate images in a folder.

commands.name: compare
commands.description: Compare two images to see their similarity.

options.name: --images
options.description: Paths of the images to compare. You can pass this '-i' parameter multiple times to compare at least two images.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed log

options.name: --path
options.description: Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)

options.name: --duplicate-similar
options.description: Similarity bar. This value means two image are considered as duplicates if their similarity is greater than it. Setting too small may cause different images to be considered as duplicates. Suggested values: [96-100] [default: 96]

options.name: --recursive
options.description: Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)

options.name: --keep
options.description: Preference for sorting images by quality to determine which to keep when duplicates are found. Available options: Colorful|GrayScale|Newest|Oldest|Smallest|Largest|HighestResolution|LowestResolution. [default: Colorful|HighestResolution|Largest|Newest]

options.name: --action
options.description: Action to take when duplicates are found. Available options: Nothing, Delete, MoveToTrash. [default: MoveToTrash]

options.name: --yes
options.description: No interactive mode. Taking action without asking for confirmation.

options.name: --extensions
options.description: Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]

options.name: --threads
options.description: Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed log

commands.name: dedup-copy
commands.description: Copy images in a folder to another folder with de-duplication. (Duplicate images in destination folder will be ignored.)

options.name: --source
options.description: Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)

options.name: --destination
options.description: Path of the folder to copy de-duplicated images.

options.name: --duplicate-similar
options.description: Similarity bar. This value means two image are considered as duplicates if their similarity is greater than it. Setting too small may cause different images to be considered as duplicates. Suggested values: [96-100] [default: 96]

options.name: --recursive
options.description: Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)

options.name: --keep
options.description: Preference for sorting images by quality to determine which to keep when duplicates are found. Available options: Colorful|GrayScale|Newest|Oldest|Smallest|Largest|HighestResolution|LowestResolution. [default: Colorful|HighestResolution|Largest|Newest]

options.name: --yes
options.description: No interactive mode. Taking action without asking for confirmation.

options.name: --extensions
options.description: Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]

options.name: --threads
options.description: Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed log

commands.name: dedup-patch
commands.description: Dedup patch will fetch all files from source directory and destination directory, and if a picture in source directory is a duplicate of a picture in destination directory, and source picture has a higher quality, then the source picture will be patched to the destination picture.

options.name: --source
options.description: Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)

options.name: --destination
options.description: Path of the folder to copy de-duplicated images.

options.name: --duplicate-similar
options.description: Similarity bar. This value means two image are considered as duplicates if their similarity is greater than it. Setting too small may cause different images to be considered as duplicates. Suggested values: [96-100] [default: 96]

options.name: --recursive
options.description: Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)

options.name: --keep
options.description: Preference for sorting images by quality to determine which to keep when duplicates are found. Available options: Colorful|GrayScale|Newest|Oldest|Smallest|Largest|HighestResolution|LowestResolution. [default: Colorful|HighestResolution|Largest|Newest]

options.name: --extensions
options.description: Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]

options.name: --threads
options.description: Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed log

commands.name: dup-top
commands.description: Find top similar images in a folder compared to a source image.

options.name: --input
options.description: Path to the source image for comparison.

options.name: --source
options.description: Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)

options.name: --recursive
options.description: Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)

options.name: --extensions
options.description: Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]

options.name: --threads
options.description: Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]

options.name: --top
options.description: Number of top similar images to return. [default: 15]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed log
